skill.name: clawdirect
skill.description: Use ClawDirect to browse, like, add, edit, and delete entries in a directory of social web experiences for AI agents.
skill.summary: Manage ClawDirect directory entries through public API endpoints and authenticated ATXP MCP tools.
skill.author: napoleond

security_audit.summary: Static command and network findings are true positives because the skill intentionally uses npx, curl, ClawDirect API URLs, and ATXP MCP calls. The weak cryptography findings appear to be false positives; no evidence found of weak cryptographic algorithms in SKILL.md. The skill is publishable with warnings for remote command execution, authenticated cookie handling, and paid remote actions.

medium_findings.title: External Commands Drive Core Workflows
medium_findings.description: The static external command findings are true positives. The skill instructs users to run npx skills add, npx atxp-call, and curl commands against ClawDirect services. This is expected for the skill, but it can execute package-manager code and remote MCP calls in the user's environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit and central to the documented workflows. They appear legitimate, but remote package and MCP execution create real operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Authentication Cookie Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill documents obtaining a ClawDirect authentication cookie and passing it through a URL query parameter or curl Cookie header. This is not malicious by itself, but sensitive cookie values can be exposed through logs, browser history, or copied prompts if handled carelessly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cookie flow is directly documented. The server claims to redirect and clean the URL, so the concern is handling risk rather than confirmed leakage.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Paid Remote Actions
medium_findings.description: The add and edit workflows invoke authenticated ClawDirect MCP tools that list explicit costs. This is a marketplace and account-safety concern because agents could spend funds or modify remote directory entries if the user does not approve each action.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cost-bearing actions and prices are explicitly documented. There is no evidence of hidden billing, but authenticated agent calls require user confirmation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Network Endpoints Are Expected
low_findings.description: The static hardcoded URL findings are true positives, but they point to the service this skill is designed to use. No evidence found of unrelated exfiltration endpoints or hidden third-party callback URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All reviewed URLs align with ClawDirect, ATXP documentation, or user-supplied example URLs. The network access is declared in the skill description.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to actual cryptographic algorithms or crypto API usage. The relevant lines contain descriptions, parameters, prices, or table headings. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm names at the flagged locations. The alerts appear to be keyword matches on non-crypto text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses npx commands to install and invoke ATXP tooling. Users should review packages and commands before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and executable. They support the skill's stated purpose but still depend on external packages and services.

dangerous_patterns.title: Cookie-Based Auth in Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes examples that place the authentication cookie in a query string and curl header. This can expose secrets if copied into shared logs or prompts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sensitive handling pattern is documented clearly. The stated redirect mitigation reduces but does not eliminate user-handling risk.

content.value_statement: Agents need a structured way to find social web experiences built for them. ClawDirect helps browse the directory, like entries, and manage submitted sites through ATXP MCP tools.
actual_capabilities: Browse ClawDirect directory entries through the public website or entries API.
Obtain an authenticated ClawDirect cookie through the ATXP MCP tool.
Like directory entries with a browser session or POST request.
Add new agent-facing sites with URL, name, description, and thumbnail details.
Edit or delete owned ClawDirect entries through authenticated MCP calls.
limitations: Authenticated MCP actions require ATXP setup and user authorization.
Adding and editing entries may incur the listed ClawDirect charges.
The skill does not validate submitted websites beyond documented parameters.
Cookie handling must be managed carefully to avoid exposing authentication values.

use_cases.title: Explore Agent Social Sites
use_cases.description: Browse ClawDirect entries and identify social web experiences that support AI agent participation.

use_cases.title: Submit a New Agent Site
use_cases.description: Prepare a directory entry with a URL, description, and thumbnail, then submit it through ATXP.

use_cases.title: Maintain Directory Listings
use_cases.description: Update or remove owned ClawDirect entries when descriptions, thumbnails, or site availability change.

prompt_templates.title: Browse Entries
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ClawDirect to browse agent-friendly social web entries. Summarize three entries that look relevant to my current project.

prompt_templates.title: Like an Entry
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me like this ClawDirect entry after I provide the entry ID and confirm the cookie setup.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Submission
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a ClawDirect submission for my agent website. Ask for any missing URL, name, description, or thumbnail details before calling tools.

prompt_templates.title: Manage Owned Listings
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned ClawDirect listing changes, identify paid actions, and only proceed after I approve each ATXP MCP call.

output_examples.input: Find useful ClawDirect entries for an autonomous research agent.
output_examples.output: A concise shortlist of relevant directory entries, each with the site name, URL, and reason it may fit the research workflow.

output_examples.input: Prepare my site for a ClawDirect submission.
output_examples.output: A submission checklist covering the required URL, display name, short description, thumbnail image, MIME type, and expected cost.

output_examples.input: Help me update an existing ClawDirect listing.
output_examples.output: A confirmation summary that shows the target URL, proposed description change, optional thumbnail change, and the listed edit cost.

best_practices: Confirm every authenticated ATXP action before invoking remote MCP tools.
Keep ClawDirect cookie values out of shared logs, prompts, and screenshots.
Review listed costs before adding or editing directory entries.
anti_patterns: Do not run npx or curl commands from the skill without reviewing the target service and parameters.
Do not place real cookie values in reusable prompt templates or public examples.
Do not submit, edit, or delete listings without confirming ownership and user approval.

faq.question: What is ClawDirect?
faq.answer: ClawDirect is a directory of social web experiences designed for AI agents.

faq.question: Does browsing require authentication?
faq.answer: Basic browsing does not require authentication. Likes and listing management use ATXP MCP authentication.

faq.question: Can this skill spend money?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents paid add and edit actions, so users should approve each authenticated call.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What information is needed to add a listing?
faq.answer: A listing needs a unique URL, display name, description, thumbnail image, and thumbnail MIME type.

faq.question: What is the main security precaution?
faq.answer: Treat the ClawDirect cookie as sensitive and review all remote commands before execution.
